new file mode 100644
index 000000000..408c9ab83
--- /dev/null
+++ b/Documentation/scheduler/sched-raptorlake-cluster.rst
@@ -0,0 +1,26 @@
+===================================================
+Raptor Lake Cluster-Aware Cache Scheduling (CAS v2)
+===================================================
+
+Overview
+========
+On Intel Core i7-14700KF (Raptor Lake) and similar hybrid architectures, the processor features a single unified L3 Last Level Cache (LLC) shared across all Performance-cores (P-cores) and Efficient-cores (E-cores). 
+
+Because traditional Cache-Aware Scheduling (CAS v4) evaluates scheduling domains at the ``SD_SHARE_LLC`` level, it perceives only a single LLC domain (``has_multi_llcs == false``), which would normally cause the kernel to disable CAS entirely.
+
+This patchset adapts CAS v2 to operate at the cluster level (``SD_CLUSTER``), utilizing ``sd_cache_id``, ``sd_cache_size``, and ``sd_cache_shared`` to manage scheduling across P-core and E-core L2 clusters.
+
+Dynamic Cache Domain Masking
+============================
+To address upstream feedback from Peter Zijlstra, CAS v2 implements dynamic cache domain masking (``mm->sc_stat.domain_mask``). Instead of tracking a single preferred cache domain, the scheduler dynamically selects a minimal set of cache domains to perfectly accommodate the process concurrency (``nr_running_avg``).
+
+For example, on a 14700KF with 3 E-core clusters (4 cores each):
+- A process with 6 threads will be assigned a domain mask covering exactly two E-core clusters (8 cores total).
+- This perfectly confines the working set, eliminating L2 cache thrashing while leaving the remaining E-core cluster and P-cores completely undisturbed.
+
+DebugFS Interface
+=================
+Task cache preference and domain masks can be inspected via ``/proc/[pid]/sched``:
+- ``sc_stat_cpu``: Primary preferred CPU
+- ``sc_llc``: Preferred cluster ID (``sd_cache_id``)
+- ``domain_mask``: Dynamic bitmap of active cache domains
